Phase-19.NAME: Anti-Ping-Pong Abuse Detection
Phase-19.goal: Replace naive endpoint-change suspicion with Redis-backed endpoint history that distinguishes normal roaming from true WireGuard/AWG ping-pong reuse, then apply soft auto-rotation only for confirmed abuse patterns.

scope.item-1: Do not change billing device limits; reuse existing per-plan device slots.
scope.item-2: Do not introduce hard account bans in this phase.
scope.item-3: Do not inspect payload traffic; only AWG peer metadata and endpoint IP history are used.
scope.item-4: Keep initial rollout safe with observe mode before auto_rotate mode is enabled in production.

Phase-19.step-1: Add anti-abuse settings: mode, scan interval, history window, TTL, ping-pong alternation threshold, unique-IP threshold and enforcement cooldown. Defaults must be safe for local tests and production observe rollout.
Phase-19.step-2: Create anti_abuse classifier with normalized IP extraction, port-change ignore rules, bounded history trimming, A-B-A-B ping-pong detection and excessive unique-IP churn detection.
Phase-19.step-3: Add Redis-backed endpoint history store with TTL plus deterministic in-memory fake for tests. Redis outage must return degraded observe-only decisions.
Phase-19.step-4: Refactor HandshakeAnomalyMonitor to call the classifier instead of treating every rapid endpoint change as abuse. Preserve last_seen, last_handshake and last_endpoint metadata writes.
Phase-19.step-5: Make detect_handshake_anomalies interval configurable in seconds and target 15 to 30 seconds for production. Ensure job registration stays idempotent.
Phase-19.step-6: Implement soft enforcement: on confirmed abuse, acquire Redis cooldown lock, record audit event, rotate logical device config and reprovision the peer so the shared old config stops working.
Phase-19.step-7: Add durable event types for ping-pong abuse detection, multi-network abuse detection, auto-rotate start, auto-rotate completion, cooldown skip and Redis degraded mode.
Phase-19.step-8: Extend admin device payload with recent anti-abuse event context so operators can see why a config was rotated.
Phase-19.step-9: Add verification for roaming, ping-pong, multi-network abuse, Redis degradation, cooldown, scheduler interval, audit events and config rotation side effects.

data-flows.DF-ANTI-001: Normal roaming: peer endpoint A to B once in the window -> classifier returns roaming -> no rotation.
data-flows.DF-ANTI-002: Ping-pong abuse: peer endpoint A to B to A to B inside the window -> audit event -> auto-rotate if mode allows.
data-flows.DF-ANTI-003: Redis degraded: history store unavailable -> log degraded marker -> persist no enforcement action.

phase-gates.gate-1: Port-only endpoint changes never trigger abuse.
phase-gates.gate-2: A single Wi-Fi to LTE transition does not rotate config.
phase-gates.gate-3: A-B-A-B endpoint alternation triggers abuse only after the configured threshold.
phase-gates.gate-4: Auto-rotation removes the old peer and keeps the same logical device identity.
phase-gates.gate-5: Redis outage does not auto-rotate any device.

execution-evidence.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_anti_ping_pong_detector.py tests/test_handshake_anomaly_detector.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_device_access_policy.py tests/test_device_audit_log.py tests/test_admin_device_control.py -v
execution-evidence.summary: 24 passed, 12 warnings on 2026-04-20. Run outside sandbox because sandboxed aiosqlite async SQLite initialization hangs.
